reasoning: There is no evidence of a new non-SARS-CoV-2 coronavirus outbreak in humans. A bat coronavirus (HKU5-CoV-2) was discovered earlier in 2025 with potential to infect humans, but no human cases have been identified. With only one month remaining in 2025 and no active outbreak underway, it is highly implausible that a new coronavirus would emerge, spread globally, and be officially designated a standalone pandemic by the World Health Organization before December 31, 2025. The Polymarket price was 1% on Nov 13, and recent news searches confirm no relevant outbreak.
